description:
You are acting as a corporate compliance-officer who has collected four single-column CSV extracts from different scanners.  
    All of these extracts have already been placed for you in the directory  

        /home/user/raw  

    with the exact file-names shown below:  

        1. /home/user/raw/hostnames.csv  
        2. /home/user/raw/os.csv  
        3. /home/user/raw/patch_status.csv  
        4. /home/user/raw/av_status.csv  

    Every file is formatted as one column of text, separated by new-lines, and **each file contains a one-line header that must be removed before further processing**.  
    Example layout (do not include the surrounding box characters):  

        ┌──────────────────────┐
        │ HOSTNAME             │  <- header line
        │ server01             │
        │ server02             │
        │ …                    │
        └──────────────────────┘

    Goal  
    ----  
    1. Create a new directory that will contain your compliance artefacts:  

           /home/user/compliance  

    2. Using standard Linux command-line utilities that manipulate columns (specifically “cut” and “paste”), build a consolidated CSV report that joins the four input files column-wise in their current order (first record of each file stays first, etc.).  
       • Use a comma ( , ) as the delimiter when merging the columns.  
       • Remove the header lines from the input files so that only real data rows are joined.  
       • Prepend a single header row to the resulting file with the exact field names and order shown below:  

             Hostname,OperatingSystem,PatchStatus,AntivirusStatus  

       • Save the finished report as  

             /home/user/compliance/final_audit.csv  

    3. Create a simple generation log named  

           /home/user/compliance/generation.log  

       The log must contain one line for **every** line that ends up inside final_audit.csv, including the header row.  
       Each line of generation.log must begin with the literal text “GENERATED: ” (note the colon and trailing space) followed by the corresponding line from final_audit.csv, **exactly as it appears in the CSV file**.  
       Example format (spacing and commas must match):  

           GENERATED: Hostname,OperatingSystem,PatchStatus,AntivirusStatus  
           GENERATED: server01,Ubuntu 20.04,Compliant,Enabled  
           …and so on for every data line…  

    Verification Expectations  
    -------------------------  
    The automated test suite will perform the following checks:  

    A. Directory /home/user/compliance exists.  
    B. File /home/user/compliance/final_audit.csv exists and contains **exactly six lines**:  
       • one header line  
       • five data lines  

       Lines must appear in the same order as the original records in /home/user/raw/hostnames.csv after its header is removed.  
       Each line must end with a single UNIX newline character (`\n`). No extra blank lines.  

    C. File /home/user/compliance/generation.log exists and contains one line for every line in final_audit.csv, prefixed with the literal string “GENERATED: ”.  
       The remainder of each log line must match the corresponding CSV line byte-for-byte.  
       The test is case-sensitive.  

    Important technical notes  
    -------------------------  
    • You do NOT have root privileges—work strictly inside the /home/user hierarchy.  
    • Do not move, rename or edit the original source files in /home/user/raw.  
    • Make sure the new directory and both output files are world-readable (chmod 644 for files, 755 for directory is fine).  
    • The only artefacts the grader cares about are final_audit.csv and generation.log in the exact locations and formats described above.  

    When you are done, the two files should be present and contain the correct, precisely formatted data so the automated tests can verify your work.
truth:
<!--
    Ground-truth data below is used by the grader and must remain secret.
    ==================================================================== -->

    <!-- Pre-existing input files and their exact contents ---------------->
    <file path="/home/user/raw/hostnames.csv"><![CDATA[
HOSTNAME
server01
server02
db01
web02
proxy01
]]></file>

    <file path="/home/user/raw/os.csv"><![CDATA[
OS
Ubuntu 20.04
CentOS 8
Ubuntu 18.04
Debian 10
AlmaLinux 9
]]></file>

    <file path="/home/user/raw/patch_status.csv"><![CDATA[
PATCH_STATUS
Compliant
Non-Compliant
Compliant
Compliant
Non-Compliant
]]></file>

    <file path="/home/user/raw/av_status.csv"><![CDATA[
AV_STATUS
Enabled
Disabled
Enabled
Enabled
Disabled
]]></file>

    <!-- Expected artefacts created by the agent ------------------------->
    <file path="/home/user/compliance/final_audit.csv" should_exist="yes"><![CDATA[
Hostname,OperatingSystem,PatchStatus,AntivirusStatus
server01,Ubuntu 20.04,Compliant,Enabled
server02,CentOS 8,Non-Compliant,Disabled
db01,Ubuntu 18.04,Compliant,Enabled
web02,Debian 10,Compliant,Enabled
proxy01,AlmaLinux 9,Non-Compliant,Disabled
]]></file>

    <file path="/home/user/compliance/generation.log" should_exist="yes"><![CDATA[
GENERATED: Hostname,OperatingSystem,PatchStatus,AntivirusStatus
GENERATED: server01,Ubuntu 20.04,Compliant,Enabled
GENERATED: server02,CentOS 8,Non-Compliant,Disabled
GENERATED: db01,Ubuntu 18.04,Compliant,Enabled
GENERATED: web02,Debian 10,Compliant,Enabled
GENERATED: proxy01,AlmaLinux 9,Non-Compliant,Disabled
]]></file>
